rationale: On one authored balanced 32-task held-out corpus and one local quantized Qwen3-8B snapshot, the full reflexive policy improved route correctness from 6 to 31 and useful outcomes from 9 to 21 relative to LLM-first, while reducing wrong fast paths from 18 to 1. It was not a Pareto improvement: the full policy had two unsafe outputs versus zero for LLM-first, and its contract verifier repaired no initially incorrect deliberation cases. The bounded routing effect is retained as a result, but no support promotion is accepted.
deliberation_disposition: Fixed deliberation corrupted three initially correct answers and repaired two; adaptive stopping corrupted one and repaired one; verifier-gated stopping preserved all twelve initially correct answers but repaired none. The fifteen historical correct-to-incorrect cases remain active regression cases without reconstructed prompts or new model calls.
non_claims: does not establish a safe or generally useful reflexive router
does not establish useful test-time scaling
does not establish evaluator independence outside internal implementation separation
does not establish transfer, deployment, SOTA, AGI, or ASI
does not add a pre-deliberative chapter
